mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 16 basis points signals an accommodative monetary stance. Sustained low rates compress long-term discount rates, reducing the required yield on 10-year Treasuries. As the Fed maintains this easing posture, Treasury yields decline over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 15 basis points over the 20-day forward window despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 25 basis points, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales at 415.7 billion dollars and housing starts at 861 thousand units indicate robust consumer demand. Strong demand signals future inflation expectations, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as investors demand higher compensation for inflation risk over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 10 basis points despite both RSAFS and HOUST remaining above their 12-month moving averages, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 7.8 percent is elevated relative to historical full-employment levels. If unemployment rises above 8.2 percent over the next 60 days, it signals economic deceleration and flight-to-safety demand for Treasuries, causing yields to fall sharply.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 8.2 percent within 60 trading days
failure_condition: UNRATE rises above 8.2 percent but 10-year Treasury yield increases by more than 20 basis points, or signal fails to predict negative returns in > 55% of triggered instances over 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 97.509 (index 2017=100) is below trend. Persistent weakness in production signals supply-side constraints and stagflationary pressures, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as investors demand higher real yields to compensate for inflation risk over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines more than 0.5 percent month-over-month
failure_condition: INDPRO declines more than 0.5 percent but 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 15 basis points, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 134.021 million shows solid labor market strength. When combined with CPI at 231.025 (indicating moderate price pressures), sustained employment growth signals the Fed will maintain or raise rates, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as the market reprices rate expectations upward over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS grows more than 200 thousand month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.3 percent month-over-month
regime_hint: tight labor market with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Both PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL trigger conditions are met but 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 10 basis points, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP at 13,652.5 billion (chained 2017 dollars) represents solid economic growth. Sustained GDP growth above trend signals the economy is operating near capacity, increasing inflation expectations and causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as investors demand higher yields to compensate for future inflation over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 grows at annualized rate above 2.5 percent but 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 25 basis points over 60-day window, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI at 231.025 combined with FEDFUNDS at 16 basis points indicates real rates are deeply negative. If CPI accelerates above 2.5 percent year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 50 basis points, the Fed faces pressure to tighten, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise sharply as the market prices in future rate increases over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.4 percent month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains below 50 basis points
failure_condition: Trigger condition met but 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 12 basis points, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts at 861 thousand units are moderating from peak levels. If housing starts fall below 800 thousand units over the next 60 days, it signals weakening demand and economic deceleration, triggering flight-to-safety flows into long-duration Treasuries and causing yields to fall sharply.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 800 thousand units within 60 trading days
regime_hint: demand deceleration or housing market weakness
failure_condition: HOUST falls below 800 thousand units but 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 20 basis points, or signal fails to predict negative returns in > 55% of triggered instances over 3-year test window.
